title: marsdenia griffithii
intro: Marsdenia griffithii, also known as the Griffith's milk vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a perennial climber native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a decoction or a tincture to be used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory and digestive issues.
